summary: Completed comprehensive testing of VasilisNetShield CMS features including Rich Text Editor, Blog CRUD, role-based access control (media_manager), and login/logout flows. All 24 backend tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tests confirmed all critical functionality working.

issues: Minor: Console warning 'Missing Description or aria-describedby for DialogContent' - accessibility improvement needed

seed_data_creation: Test blog posts created during testing (prefixed with TEST_)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both test users exist and working. Backend API tests in /app/backend/tests/test_content_api.py cover content CRUD, authentication, and RBAC. Rich Text Editor uses contentEditable with execCommand for formatting.

rich_text_editor.details: Custom contentEditable-based editor working. Toolbar buttons for H1, H2, Bold, Italic, Underline, Lists, Quote, Code, Link, Image all present. Blog post creation with formatted content successful.

blog_crud.details: Create, Read, Update, Delete all working via Content Manager. Public blog page displays published posts correctly with author, date, tags, and excerpts.

role_based_access_control.details: media_manager role correctly restricted to Content, Training, Certificates navigation only. Cannot see Dashboard, Users, Settings, Organizations, Campaigns, etc. Can create blog posts and manage CMS content.

login_logout.details: Login redirects super_admin to /dashboard, media_manager to /training. Logout clears session and redirects to /auth.

public_blog_page.details: Blog page at /blog displays all published posts in grid layout with proper styling, tags, and author attribution.

navigation.details: super_admin sees all 13 nav items. media_manager sees only 3 allowed items (Content, Training, Certificates).
